---
title: "blocky benchmark report"
output: github_document
params:
  data_file: "benchmark_data.tsv"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6, dpi = 150)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r load-data}
df <- read_tsv(params$data_file) %>%
  mutate(
    test   = factor(test),
    mode   = factor(mode),
    format = factor(format)
  )
```

```{r compute-metrics}
# Split scaling tests from scatter tests
df_scaling <- df %>% filter(test != "scatter")
df_scatter <- df %>% filter(test == "scatter")

# Baseline: median wall time per test x format (native, ncpus == 1)
baselines <- df_scaling %>%
  filter(mode == "native", ncpus == 1) %>%
  group_by(test, format) %>%
  summarise(wall_baseline = median(wall_seconds), .groups = "drop")

# Scaling results: join baselines and compute metrics
results <- df_scaling %>%
  left_join(baselines, by = c("test", "format")) %>%
  group_by(test, mode, ncpus, nthreads, format) %>%
  summarise(
    wall_median    = median(wall_seconds),
    wall_min       = min(wall_seconds),
    wall_max       = max(wall_seconds),
    rss_median     = median(peak_rss_mb),
    rss_min        = min(peak_rss_mb),
    rss_max        = max(peak_rss_mb),
    wall_baseline  = first(wall_baseline),
    n_reps         = n(),
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    speedup        = wall_baseline / wall_median,
    speedup_min    = wall_baseline / wall_max,
    speedup_max    = wall_baseline / wall_min,
    efficiency     = speedup / ncpus * 100,
    efficiency_min = speedup_min / ncpus * 100,
    efficiency_max = speedup_max / ncpus * 100
  )

# Scatter results: compare index types
# Wall times are divided by 5 (5 internal reps per process invocation)
scatter_results <- df_scatter %>%
  mutate(wall_seconds = wall_seconds / 5) %>%
  group_by(mode) %>%
  summarise(
    wall_median = median(wall_seconds),
    wall_min    = min(wall_seconds),
    wall_max    = max(wall_seconds),
    rss_median  = median(peak_rss_mb),
    rss_min     = min(peak_rss_mb),
    rss_max     = max(peak_rss_mb),
    n_reps      = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    index_type = str_replace(mode, "scatter_", ""),
    index_type = factor(index_type, levels = c("csi", "tbi", "gzi", "none"))
  )
```

```{r plot-theme}
mode_lines <- c(
  "native"    = "dashed",
  "blocky"    = "solid"
)

theme_bench <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Speedup

```{r speedup-plot}
ggplot(results, aes(x = ncpus, y = speedup, colour = mode, linetype = mode)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = speedup_min, ymax = speedup_max), width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = mode_lines) +
  facet_grid(test ~ format) +
  labs(x = "CPUs", y = "Speedup (vs baseline)", colour = "Mode", linetype = "Mode") +
  theme_bench
```

## Parallel efficiency

Efficiency = speedup / ncpus x 100%. A value of 100% means perfect linear scaling.

```{r efficiency-plot}
ggplot(results, aes(x = ncpus, y = efficiency, colour = mode, linetype = mode)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = efficiency_min, ymax = efficiency_max), width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = mode_lines) +
  facet_grid(test ~ format) +
  labs(x = "CPUs", y = "Efficiency (%)", colour = "Mode", linetype = "Mode") +
  theme_bench
```

## Wall time

```{r walltime-plot}
ggplot(results, aes(x = ncpus, y = wall_median, colour = mode, linetype = mode)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = wall_min, ymax = wall_max), width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = mode_lines) +
  facet_grid(test ~ format, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "CPUs", y = "Wall time (seconds)", colour = "Mode", linetype = "Mode") +
  theme_bench
```

## Peak RSS

```{r rss-plot}
ggplot(results, aes(x = ncpus, y = rss_median, colour = mode, linetype = mode)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rss_min, ymax = rss_max), width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = mode_lines) +
  facet_grid(test ~ format, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "CPUs", y = "Peak RSS (MB)", colour = "Mode", linetype = "Mode") +
  theme_bench
```

## Scatter: index format comparison

Wall time for `blocky scatter -n 4` by index type. CSI and TBI use index-based splitting; GZI uses BGZF block offsets; none uses blocky's scan mechanism.

```{r scatter-wall-plot, eval=nrow(scatter_results) > 0}
ggplot(scatter_results, aes(x = index_type, y = wall_median, fill = index_type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = wall_min, ymax = wall_max), width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.4) +
  labs(x = "Index type", y = "Wall time (seconds)", fill = "Index") +
  theme_bench +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r scatter-rss-plot, eval=nrow(scatter_results) > 0}
ggplot(scatter_results, aes(x = index_type, y = rss_median, fill = index_type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rss_min, ymax = rss_max), width = 0.2, linewidth = 0.4) +
  labs(x = "Index type", y = "Peak RSS (MB)", fill = "Index") +
  theme_bench +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
